title: Longest subarray with a given sum

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers numsnumsnums with length nnn and an integer kkk. Your task is to determine the length of the longest continuous sub-array whose elements sum up exactly to kkk. If there is no such sub-array, return 000.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**longestSubarraySumlongestSubarraySumlongestSubarraySum ‚Äì This function computes the length of the longest continuous sub-array whose sum is exactly equal to kkk.

**Parameters**
arrarrarr : A vector of integers of length nnn, representing the array.
kkk : An integer representing the required target sum.
**Return Value**
Returns a single integer ‚Äî
the **maximum length** of any continuous sub-array whose sum equals kkk.
If no such sub-array exists, the function returns 000.
`The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.`

**Constraints**
1‚â§T‚â§1051 \leq T \leq 10^51‚â§T‚â§105
1‚â§n‚â§1051 \leq n \leq 10^51‚â§n‚â§105
‚àí105‚â§arr[i]‚â§105-10^5 \leq arr[i] \leq 10^5‚àí105‚â§arr[i]‚â§105
‚àí109‚â§k‚â§109-10^9 \leq k \leq 10^9‚àí109‚â§k‚â§109

Input Format


The first line of input will contain a single integer **TTT**, denoting the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.



The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers **nnn** and **kkk** ‚Äî the length of the array and the required sum respectively.
The next line contains **nnn** space-separated integers, representing the array **`nums`**.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer ‚Äî
the **length of the longest continuous subarray whose sum is exactly kkk**.
If no such subarray exists, output **0**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case the sub-array [5, 2, 7, 1] has a total sum of 15 and is the longest one with this sum. It starts at index 1 and ends at index 4, so its length is 4.
In the second test case there is no continuous sub-array in the array that adds up to 6. Hence, the result is 0.

solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n) (one pass through the array).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) (to store prefix sums in the map).
